data.narrative:
Unseenlabs, a Series C-stage space and aerospace company, is evaluated against a peer set of five same-sector, same-stage private companies: Axiom Space, Impulse Space, Varda Space Industries, Skyryse, and Isar Aerospace. This cohort spans valuations from $1.0B to $2.5B.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach, given Unseenlabs' private status and undisclosed revenue. The five comparable companies carry a median valuation of $1.5B, with Axiom Space at the high end ($2.5B) and Isar Aerospace at the low end ($1.0B). This method directly anchors stage and sector alignment and produces an implied valuation of $1.5B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied; while the Space & Aerospace sector (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) trades at a median 59.8x price-to-sales, Unseenlabs' revenue is not disclosed.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.3B to $1.7B, with a median of $1.5B. This range reflects the empirical spread within the peer cohort. Key uncertainties include the company's revenue scale relative to peers, unit economics, and customer concentration—data not available in this assessment. The single-method reliance also constrains analytical depth; public comps could narrow the range if Unseenlabs' financial performance were disclosed.
